title: poa lhasaensis
intro: Poa lhasaensis, also known as the Tibetan meadow grass, is a type of grass species found in the high-altitude regions of the Himalayas and Tibetan Plateau. It has been traditionally used in Tibetan medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and promoting wound healing. Poa lhasaensis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used in traditional Tibetan medicine to treat various ailments.
